titleStmt.title: SII 17.212: original edition by K. G. Krishnan
No. 212. (A.R. No. 190 of 1904.) TIRUVAKKARAI, VILUPPURAM TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.

summary.p: On the south wall of the 1000 pillared maṇḍapa inside the second prākāra of the Chandramauḷīśvara temple.

This inscription is dated in the 2nd Year of Kulōttuṅgachōḻadēva who was pleased to conquer Madurai and the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya, i.e., Kulōttuṅga III. The donor evidently belongs to the Śambuvarāya family and his title Pāṇḍināḍu-koṇḍān seems to indicate that he participated in the campaign against the Pāṇḍyas.

This records the building of a maṇḍapam, named Gaṇḍar Śūriyan-tirumaṇḍapam in the temple of god Āḷuḍaiyār at Tiruvakkarai in Māttūr-nāḍu in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu by Ammai Appaṉ Pāṇḍināḍu-koṇḍāṉ alias Gaṇḍar Sūriyaṉ Śambuvarāyaṉ.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tribhuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ maturaiyum pāṇṭiyaṉ muṭittalaiyuṅ koṇṭa ruḷiṉa śrīkulottu
ṅka coḻadevaṟku yāṇṭu 2 vatu Irājarājavaḷanāṭṭu māttūr nāṭṭu Uṭaiyār tiruvakkarai Āḷu ṭaiyār
koyilil Ittirumaṇṭapam kaṇṭar cūriyaṉ tirumaṇṭapam Eṉṉum peyarā
l ceyvittāṉ Ammai Appaṉ pāṇṭināṭu koṇṭānāṉ kaṇṭar cūri
yaṉ campuvarāyaṉ .
Digital edition of SII 17.212 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
